heading: Exclusive right to name, emblems, and badges
label: 21705 Exclusive right to name, emblems, and badges
text: The corporation and its State and local subdivisions have the exclusive right to use the name “The American Legion” or “American Legion”. The corporation has the exclusive right to use, manufacture, and control the right to manufacture, emblems and badges the corporation adopts.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2170536:44 (words between 4th and 5th semicolons)., §§ 4 (words between 4th and 5th semicolons), 8, ; , .Sept. 16, 1919, ch. 5941 Stat. 285June 26, 1953, ch. 15367 Stat. 8236:48.The word “sole” is omitted as included in “exclusive”. The words “to have and”, “in carrying out its purposes”, and “in carrying out the purposes of the corporation” are omitted as unnecessary.
